55bbd599: The archive page is a section of a blog that displays all posts in chronological order, typically presented in a timeline format, making it easy for readers to browse and search for historical articles.
6f42f917: The archive page template is a `.archive.ts` file located in the `.kecare/` directory. The generator will call it after processing all articles to generate the archive page.
76b38bd4: `.archive.ts` needs to return an object containing `fsPath` and `template`. The generator will use this information to create the archive page. Unlike landing pages, archive pages typically do not require pagination; all articles are displayed on a single page, grouped by year.

8ab5b12e: Unlike landing pages, the data structure of archive pages is more streamlined, containing only the essential information needed for archive display, which helps reduce the size of the generated pages.
